project_description: Strategic positioning, content creation, and narrative development for launching Sandbox-GTM as a go-to-market platform focused on event-driven pipeline and revenue impact.
domain_knowledge: Sandbox-GTM positions events as a first-class go-to-market channel rather than a logistics exercise.
Traditional event platforms primarily measure attendance and registration metrics rather than downstream GTM impact.
Sandbox-GTM focuses on analytics such as acquisition health, engagement signals, and revenue impact tied to events.
The platform philosophy is that events should connect directly to pipeline, sales follow-up, and revenue attribution.
Enterprise event marketing teams struggle to prove ROI from events because systems are fragmented across event platforms, CRM, and post-event workflows.
AI features in event platforms typically optimize tasks inside the platform but do not solve cross-functional GTM coordination issues.
Event-led growth requires alignment between marketing, sales, and operations systems.
Sandbox-GTM’s narrative emphasizes what happens during and after events rather than just getting people to attend.
Forge by Sandbox is the product engineering arm building software products related to event infrastructure.
Event Connect is the first Forge by Sandbox product and provides branded kiosk check-in stations syncing attendee data with HubSpot or Google Sheets in real time.

decisions_made.decision: Position Sandbox-GTM as a GTM intelligence layer for events rather than a logistics platform.
decisions_made.rationale: The core differentiation is proving pipeline and revenue impact rather than managing registration and attendance.

decisions_made.decision: Launch Sandbox-GTM publicly through a founder-led LinkedIn announcement.
decisions_made.rationale: Founder narrative creates authenticity and positions the company as a category challenger.

decisions_made.decision: Use editorial, sophisticated photography for visual branding rather than illustrations or cartoon-style imagery.
decisions_made.rationale: The brand aesthetic aims to feel premium, strategic, and enterprise-ready.

decisions_made.decision: Anchor messaging around questions event teams actually need answered: engagement, outcomes, and revenue.
decisions_made.rationale: This framing highlights the limitations of traditional event platform analytics.

decisions_made.decision: Keep mentions of Sandbox in industry commentary minimal and contextual rather than overtly promotional.
decisions_made.rationale: Thought leadership should feel credible and not sales-driven.

decisions_made.decision: Build credibility through industry influencers and thought leadership collaboration.
decisions_made.rationale: Industry validation accelerates trust and distribution in the event tech ecosystem.

preferences.value: Editorial, sophisticated, human photography with moody professional lighting
Minimal promotion, strong insight-led thought leadership
Clean, modern, enterprise-grade visuals aligned with a premium SaaS feel
Short, punchy founder posts and thought leadership commentary

patterns_that_worked: Framing industry problems through questions event teams actually care about.
Contrasting traditional event platform metrics with GTM impact metrics.
Using founder voice posts on LinkedIn to establish thought leadership.
Combining strategic commentary with minimal references to Sandbox as the solution.
Creating editorial-style imagery that reflects strategy sessions rather than live event environments.
mistakes_to_avoid: Positioning Sandbox as an add-on to traditional registration platforms.
Using cartoonish or illustrative imagery for brand visuals.
Overtly selling the platform in thought leadership content.
Including onsite event environments when visuals are meant to represent strategic planning.
Messaging that focuses only on event logistics instead of GTM impact.
current_state: Sandbox-GTM is fully live in production at sandbox-gtm.com. The app is deployed on Render with Clerk production auth, Neon PostgreSQL, and full super admin access confirmed. The public narrative and thought leadership positioning is underway with founder-led LinkedIn posts, a teaser video, and an article titled 'Event Platform Analytics vs. GTM: Which Is Better?'. Early ecosystem relationships are forming. The platform is now ready to onboard users via invite codes.
next_steps: Publish and promote the article 'Event Platform Analytics vs. GTM: Which Is Better?' with a refined editorial hero image.
Develop additional thought leadership posts reinforcing the GTM narrative around events.
Explore a joint thought leadership opportunity with Intel around event-led pipeline growth.
Engage with the event tech founder community for partnerships and introductions.
Schedule a product walkthrough with key industry influencers.
open_questions: How Sandbox-GTM will formally package and price its platform offering.
Whether Intel will participate in a public thought leadership collaboration.
What distribution channels beyond LinkedIn will be used to scale the Sandbox-GTM narrative.
How the product roadmap will evolve beyond analytics and attribution capabilities.
How Forge by Sandbox products like Event Connect will integrate into the broader Sandbox-GTM ecosystem.

engineering_state.summary: Sandbox-GTM app is fully deployed to production at sandbox-gtm.com via Render. Clerk production auth is live. Super admin (brian@makemysandbox.com) confirmed working. Drizzle schema synced to Neon. GitHub repo up to date on main.
engineering_state.stack: React + Vite (client), Express + TypeScript (server), Drizzle ORM + Neon PostgreSQL, Clerk auth, Wouter routing, Radix UI + Tailwind

notes: VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY must be set in Render env vars — it is baked into the frontend bundle at build time
Clear Render build cache if packages seem missing after adding new dependencies
Clerk production instance locked to sandbox-gtm.com domain — onrender.com URL will not work with production keys

key_files.server/clerkAuth.ts: New auth file — clerkMiddleware, isAuthenticated middleware, lazy user upsert from Clerk API.
key_files.client/src/App.tsx: ClerkTokenSync component registers getToken getter for all API requests. Sign-in/sign-up routes use simple paths (not wildcards).
key_files.client/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: Gates /api/auth/user query on clerkLoaded && isSignedIn from useClerkAuth(). isAuthenticated = clerkLoaded && isSignedIn && !!user.
key_files.client/src/lib/queryClient.ts: Injects Clerk JWT as Authorization: Bearer header on all API requests via clerkTokenGetter module variable.
key_files.client/src/pages/sign-in.tsx: Clerk SignIn with routing=hash (NOT routing=path — causes Wouter sub-path conflicts)
key_files.client/src/pages/sign-up.tsx: Clerk SignUp with routing=hash (NOT routing=path — causes Wouter sub-path conflicts)
key_files.vite.config.ts: resolve.alias points @clerk/react directly to node_modules/@clerk/react/dist/index.mjs to fix Render resolution. optimizeDeps includes @clerk/react.

critical_gotchas: Use routing=hash on Clerk components — routing=path navigates to sub-paths like /sign-up/verify-email-address that Wouter cannot match, causing immediate redirect to Landing
Clerk JWT must be sent as Authorization: Bearer header — cookie-based auth alone is unreliable with @clerk/express v2
Super admin check is email domain based: any @makemysandbox.com email gets super admin automatically (routes.ts:44)

vars_required: VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (frontend — baked in at build time)

notes: Port 5000 blocked on macOS by AirPlay Receiver — always use PORT=3001
reusePort removed from httpServer.listen (ENOTSUP on macOS)
OpenAI client is lazy-initialized via getOpenAI() — no crash if key missing
Local dev uses Clerk test keys; production uses live keys

cta_architecture.request_access_landing: Hero + footer CTA → LeadFormDialog (HubSpot lead gen — intentional)
cta_architecture.request_access_pricing: Pricing page → /signup → invite code redemption page

key_terminology.definition: A platform and strategic framework focused on connecting events directly to go-to-market pipeline and revenue outcomes.
A strategy where events act as a primary driver of pipeline, engagement, and revenue within a company's go-to-market motion.
Metrics that evaluate how effectively events attract and engage target buyers.
Behavioral data from event participation that indicates buyer interest and intent.
The measurable pipeline and revenue outcomes attributable to event activity.
The product engineering arm responsible for building software products related to event infrastructure.
A Forge by Sandbox product that provides branded kiosk-based event check-in stations integrated with HubSpot or Google Sheets.
